title: HTTP Client Ownership, Context, Status, Body Limits, and Cleanup · Goroutine Forensics
summary: A preserved Go Pokedex run contains one plausible command, parsing, URL, JSON, client, cache, ownership, goroutine, cancellation, persistence, security, test, packaging, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in reusable bounded context-aware PokéAPI client and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, a…
objectives: Inject and reuse one configured http.Client or narrow transport port because transports retain connection state and support concurrent use.
Create requests with caller-owned context and derive request deadlines without hiding cancellation behind Background.
Classify success, not found, client, server, redirect, and unexpected status before domain decoding and retain bounded error evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Pokedex Go Client Reuse
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Go team handles case 1760 during a narrow-terminal ranger workflow. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove client reuse, response admission, bounded bodies, context cancellation, and cleanup; browser code must remain deterministic and side-effect-free; then change one resource identity, pagination link, JSON field, status, or body budget and revalidate admission. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Inject and reuse one configured http.Client or narrow transport port because transports retain connection state and support concurrent use.

Inspect the Pokedex Go Client Reuse trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Pokedex product engineering and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Pokedex Go Client Reuse.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Pokedex Go Roundtripper…
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: pokedex-go-roundtripper-tests-nistsu" that uses case 5395 to prove client reuse, response admission, bounded bodies, context cancellation, and cleanup. Keep defaults runnable and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact PokéAPI, read or write host files, handle real terminal or OS signals, spawn unbounded work, or claim race, leak, load, accessibility, cross-platform, package, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, original fixtures, controlled RoundTrippers and httptest servers, race and fuzz runs, temporary files, subprocess and signal harnesses, inspected artifacts, representative platforms, accessibility review, capacity, rollback, recovery, and release gates.
